# Growth Chart Calculator

A free, open-source, client-side calculator that converts a child's weight or length/height measurement into a growth percentile using WHO or CDC reference data. All computation happens in the browser — no data is uploaded and nothing is stored on any server.

## How to use

- Open https://growthchartcalculator.com/
- Enter sex, birth date, measurement date, and weight (kg) and/or length/height (cm)
- The page renders the percentile at the measurement and a growth curve showing the 3rd, 5th, 10th, 25th, 50th, 75th, 90th, 95th, and 97th percentile bands

## What the calculator supports

- Weight-for-age (0–240 months)
- Length-for-age (lying, 0–24 months) and stature/height-for-age (standing, 24+ months)
- WHO reference tables (0–60 months)
- CDC reference tables (0–240 months)
- An `auto` mode that uses WHO under 24 months and CDC at 24 months and older, matching common clinical practice

## Methodology

- Conversion uses the LMS method (Cole & Green, 1992). Z-scores are computed with the Box-Cox form when L ≠ 0 and the log form when |L| < 1e-8.
- Percentiles come from the standard normal CDF (Abramowitz & Stegun 26.2.17); inverse CDF for percentile bands uses Beasley-Springer-Moro.
- Age in months is computed as days / 30.4375.
- WHO CSVs (split by sex) and CDC CSVs (combined-sex with Sex column) are normalized to a single (month, L, M, S) shape before interpolation.

## Privacy

- 100% client-side. No backend, no analytics on measurement data, no cookies tied to inputs.
- Form state is saved only in the user's own browser localStorage so the page reloads with their last values.

## License

- MIT license. Reference data originates from WHO and CDC public releases.
